rationale: This reaction shows the formation of an imino ether hydrochloride salt (a Pinner salt) from a simple aliphatic nitrile (acetonitrile) and an alcohol (ethanol) in the presence of dry hydrogen chloride. This represents the core transformation of the Pinner reaction, which is the acid-catalyzed addition of an alcohol to a nitrile. The reactants and product are simple, acyclic, and contain the essential functional groups without extraneous complexity.
collision_avoidance_note: The chosen example uses simple, acyclic aliphatic substrates (acetonitrile, ethanol) to avoid overlap with reactions involving aromatic rings (e.g., Friedel-Crafts, Gattermann) or more complex functional groups. It specifically depicts the formation of the characteristic imino ether salt intermediate, which is central to the Pinner reaction and distinct from direct nitrile hydrolysis to amides or carboxylic acids.
